text.title: Adobe Firefly Capabilities and Rules
text.summary: Firefly feature map per Creative Cloud application, commercial framework, and key API constraints.

rule.statement: Use Firefly when commercial IP safety is required; document the generation ID alongside each asset.
rule.rationale: Firefly is trained on licensed content with clear usage terms. Generation IDs enable audit trails and regeneration if model versions change. Storing IDs prevents orphaned assets.

rule.statement: Always include a human review checkpoint before promoting any Firefly asset to production.
rule.rationale: Generated images frequently need manual refinement to match brand-specific visual language. Automated selection of generated images for production is not acceptable.

rule.statement: Pre-define a prompt template approved by the brand team; agents fill variables, never freeform prompts.
rule.rationale: Brand-specific style prompts are not reproducible across model updates. Pre-approved templates constrain variation to acceptable range and prevent prompt injection into the commercial asset pipeline.

antipattern.description: Storing presigned URLs from the API instead of downloading immediately.
antipattern.reason: Generated image presigned URLs expire in approximately 1 hour. Always download immediately and write to stable storage.

antipattern.description: Trusting empty outputs array as a success.
antipattern.reason: Content moderation blocks can return HTTP 200 with an empty outputs array. Always validate that outputs length is greater than 0 before treating a call as successful.

antipattern.description: Passing user-provided freeform text directly to Firefly.
antipattern.reason: Unsanitized user input can trigger moderation blocks or inject unintended prompt content into a commercial asset pipeline. Sanitize and route through the pre-approved template only.
